section: Class 10 Economics - Chapter 3: Money and Credit

notes.summary: The barter system was a system of exchange where goods were exchanged for other goods without using money. It had the problem of double coincidence of wants.
notes.importance: Helps understand why money was introduced in the economy.

notes.summary: Money acts as a medium of exchange, unit of account and store of value. It makes buying and selling easier.
notes.importance: Explains the importance of money in daily life transactions.

notes.summary: Modern forms of money include currency notes, coins and demand deposits. Demand deposits are money kept in banks that can be withdrawn anytime.
notes.importance: Helps understand how banking system works.

notes.summary: Credit means borrowing money from a lender with the promise to repay it later along with interest.
notes.importance: Important to understand how loans help in business and farming.
quick_facts: Used for business and personal needs

notes.summary: Formal credit is provided by banks and cooperatives under RBI supervision. Informal credit is provided by moneylenders and traders and often charges high interest.
notes.importance: Highlights differences in safety and regulation of loans.
quick_facts: Informal credit may cause debt trap

notes.summary: A debt trap occurs when a borrower keeps taking loans to repay old loans due to high interest rates.
notes.importance: Explains risks of borrowing from informal sources.
